meta_note: English translations for docs_guide.json. Keyed by inciso ID then field name.

prioridades.critica: Without this document certification is not viable.
prioridades.alta: Absence creates a material gap — strongly recommended.
prioridades.media: Maturity indicator — absence generates an observation.

proof_of_reserves_audit.descricao: Independent reserve verification with Merkle tree, on-chain vs. liabilities comparison, attestation report and Skynet badge. Modalities: one-time or Continuous Living PoR (real-time dashboard).

pentest.descricao: Advanced attack simulation on off-chain components and Web3 applications: APIs, client portal, admin panels and infrastructure. Coverage of 500+ applications and 5,000+ APIs.

smart_contract_audit.descricao: Security assessment of smart contracts and blockchain code, combining expert manual review, AI and formal verification. Identifies vulnerabilities and delivers a public report with findings and remediations.

skyinsights.descricao: Compliance, AML/CFT and risk management platform: real-time transaction monitoring, on-chain address analysis (300M+ labels), KYT (Know Your Transaction), alerts and risk reports. Multi-chain coverage.

dlt_security_solutions.descricao: Institutional strategy, architecture design and security assessments for DLT: on-chain systems, off-chain infrastructure and regulatory readiness. For exchanges, custodians and banks.

security_review.descricao: Independent review of security, compliance or market surveillance programs by the CertiK expert team. Issues a formal report with findings and recommendations.

I_a.resumo: Demonstrate that client virtual assets (VA) are strictly separated from the VASP's own VA, individually identified and protected from the VASP's own liabilities.
I_a.gatilho: Triggered by own or outsourced custody; mandatory for custodian and broker/exchange tracks.

I_a_1.descricao: Formal document approved by the board defining the segregation model, limits on client VA usage and responsibilities.
I_a_1.justificativa_legal: Art. 29, III of Res. 520: the VASP must maintain records that allow identification, at any time, of which VA belong to each client. Without a formal policy there is no way to demonstrate to the BCB that the process is systematic and supervisable.
conteudo_minimo: Client identification model (omnibus, individual wallets or mixed)
5% limit on client VA usage (art. 30, §2) and associated controls
Express prohibitions from arts. 29–31 (pledge, mix, use without consent)
Responsible party and minimum annual review frequency
Board approval with effective date and version control

I_a_2.titulo: Fund Flow Diagram — Own vs. Client VA Separation
I_a_2.descricao: Visual representation of on-chain wallets (hot, warm, cold) and internal records linking each wallet or balance to the owning client.
I_a_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 29, III: the wallet and record structure must be identifiable. A diagram demonstrates the segregation architecture to the auditor directly and verifiably.
conteudo_minimo: VASP's own VA wallets identified and separated from client wallets
Deposit, custody and withdrawal flow with reconciliation nodes
Omnibus model: sub-ledger map per client
Sub-custodian identification (if applicable) and responsibilities

I_a_3.descricao: Reconciliation report between the internal sub-ledger and on-chain positions, with a recent date, anonymised for presentation.
I_a_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 29, III: identify at any time which VA belong to each client. Reconciliation is the operational control that gives substance to the policy. Without it, segregation is merely declaratory.
conteudo_minimo: Total client VA in sub-ledger vs. total on-chain (per currency)
Permitted difference (tolerance) and treatment of discrepancies
Minimum frequency: daily for critical systems, weekly at minimum
Responsible party for execution and reconciliation approval

I_a_4.descricao: Periodic report (internal or with external auditor) proving the VASP holds on-chain the VA corresponding to declared client positions.
I_a_4.justificativa_legal: Clause I(b) of IN 701 requires proof of reserves mechanisms. The existence of a report (internal or external) proves the mechanism operates in production, not just in the policy.
conteudo_minimo: Methodology for calculating eligible reserves
Reference period and issuance date
Discrepancies found and treatment
Auditor or team responsible for verification

I_a_5.descricao: Dashboard or report evidencing the monitoring of the percentage of client VA eventually used in authorised operations.
I_a_5.justificativa_legal: Art. 30, §3: the VASP must maintain documentation available for BCB supervision. If the VASP uses client VA within the legal limit, the absence of documented controls is itself a violation.
conteudo_minimo: Total client VA under custody (denominator)
Amount used in authorised operations (numerator)
Calculated percentage and 5% limit
Client consent or declaration of non-use

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP operates with individual client wallets (non-omnibus), automatic daily reconciliation between sub-ledger and on-chain with real-time alerts for discrepancies, and publishes monthly a proof of reserves verified by an independent external auditor. The 5% limit is monitored by an automated system with automatic blocking at 4%. The entire process is approved by the board and reviewed semi-annually.
indicadores: Automatic daily reconciliation with zero unaddressed discrepancies
Monthly proof of reserves published by an external auditor
Individual wallets per client (no omnibus)
Automatic alert at 4% of the art. 30 §2 limit
Policy reviewed semi-annually by the board

I_b.resumo: Maintain continuous mechanisms demonstrating that VA declared to clients exist on-chain and are under the VASP's or sub-custodian's control.
I_b.gatilho: Mandatory for custodian and broker/exchange tracks; triggered for intermediaries with own custody.

I_b_1.descricao: Document defining the calculation method — which on-chain assets are eligible as reserves, how client positions (liabilities) are calculated and how reconciliation is performed.
I_b_1.justificativa_legal: Without a documented methodology, proof of reserves is just a number without traceability. The BCB cannot validate what has no defined procedure.
conteudo_minimo: Definition of eligible reserve assets (cold, warm, hot wallets)
Liability calculation (client positions)
Calculation and publication frequency
Treatment of locked or staked assets
Responsible party and approval

I_b_2.descricao: Most recent proof of reserves report, internal or produced by an independent auditor, demonstrating coverage >= 100% of client positions.
I_b_2.justificativa_legal: Proof of reserves must be verifiable — a periodic report (ideally signed by an auditor) is evidence that the mechanism actually operates.
conteudo_minimo: Reference date and VA covered
On-chain verification method (addresses or Merkle tree)
Total coverage: client positions vs. reserves
Findings and corrective measures if coverage < 100%

I_b_3.titulo: Proof of Reserves Disclosure Policy for Clients
I_b_3.descricao: Defines how, when and in what format the result of the proof of reserves is made available to the client and/or the public.
I_b_3.justificativa_legal: §1, II and par1_I must be accessible to the client. Publishing proof of reserves is regulatory best practice that reinforces trust and demonstrates proactive compliance.
conteudo_minimo: Publication channel (portal, email, public page)
Minimum disclosure frequency
Level of detail available to client vs. public

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP publishes monthly Merkle tree Proof-of-Reserves verifiable on-chain, with quarterly attestation by a recognised external auditor (Big 4 or crypto specialist). Each client can verify their position individually via their address hash. The result is published on the public page before login.
indicadores: Merkle tree Proof-of-Reserves verifiable on-chain by any client
Quarterly external attestation by an independent auditor
Monthly public disclosure of results
Coverage >= 102% (safety buffer above 100%)

II.resumo: Control and document all critical third-party service providers, especially processing, storage and cloud, ensuring technical capacity, SLA, auditability and an exit plan.
II.gatilho: Triggered when the VASP outsources critical data processing or cloud infrastructure to third parties.

II_1.descricao: Centralised list of all providers that process, store or have access to critical VASP or client data or VA.
II_1.justificativa_legal: Arts. 32 and 33 of Res. 520 require control of relevant services. Without an updated inventory it is impossible to demonstrate the VASP knows and controls its critical dependencies.
conteudo_minimo: Provider name, function, criticality and headquarters
Date of last assessment / due diligence
Internal relationship owner
Compliance status and next review

II_2.titulo: Contracts with Regulatory Clauses (SLA, Audit, Exit)
II_2.descricao: Contract excerpts or summary of SLA, audit access and exit/transition procedure clauses for each relevant provider.
II_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 32: contracts with relevant providers must ensure continuity, auditability and orderly exit. Without specific clauses, the BCB has no supervision guarantee.
conteudo_minimo: Availability SLA and response time
Audit clause or BCB inspection right
Termination procedure and data portability
Incident notification within a defined timeframe
Jurisdiction and applicable law

II_3.descricao: Formal policy defining selection criteria, due diligence, continuous monitoring and replacement of critical service providers.
II_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 43, VIII of Res. 520: governance policies must include rules for selecting, contracting and monitoring relevant providers.
conteudo_minimo: Eligibility criteria and pre-contract due diligence
Continuous monitoring process (frequency, KPIs)
Disqualification / replacement criteria
Exit plan for each critical provider

II_4.descricao: Annual evaluation report for each relevant provider, including SLA performance, incidents and improvement plan.
II_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 33: the VASP must continuously monitor the adequacy of providers. An annually documented review is the minimum expected to demonstrate effective control.
conteudo_minimo: SLA assessment for the period
Relevant incidents and resolutions
Result of periodic due diligence
Decision: maintain, require improvement or replace

II_5.descricao: Checklist or report of due diligence conducted before contracting each relevant provider in the last 24 months.
II_5.justificativa_legal: Art. 32: contracting must be preceded by technical and operational assessment. Without evidence of due diligence, the BCB cannot assess that the provider choice was diligent.
conteudo_minimo: Technical and operational capacity verified
Regulatory status and licences
Declared incident history
Result and contracting decision

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP maintains an automated Third-Party Risk Management (TPRM) system with continuous risk assessment, real-time SLA monitoring, quarterly reports per provider and annually tested exit plans. All contracts include a BCB auditability clause and incident notification within 2 hours.
indicadores: 100% of critical providers with annually reviewed contracts
Real-time SLA monitoring with automated alerts
Documented due diligence before each contracting
Tested exit plan for the 3 most critical providers
Quarterly provider performance report

III.resumo: Ensure that relevant service providers based outside Brazil have technical, operational and legal capacity, and that the BCB retains supervisory access.
III.gatilho: Triggered when the VASP contracts a relevant provider headquartered outside Brazil.

III_1.titulo: Due Diligence Dossier per Foreign Provider
III_1.descricao: Set of documents per foreign provider: local licences, financial capacity, incident history, data structure and BCB access.
III_1.justificativa_legal: Art. 83 of Res. 520 requires assessment of the foreign provider's technical, operational and legal capacity. Without an individual dossier, control is merely declaratory.
conteudo_minimo: Regulatory licences in the home jurisdiction
Verified operational capacity (infrastructure, SLA, team)
Declared incident and sanction history
BCB access mechanism to records and data

III_2.titulo: BCB Access Clause and Jurisdiction in Contracts
III_2.descricao: Contract excerpt with the foreign provider demonstrating BCB access clauses, Brazilian jurisdiction for regulatory purposes and incident notification.
III_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 83: contracts with foreign providers must guarantee BCB access for supervision. Without this clause the contract is irregular under Res. 520.
conteudo_minimo: BCB access clause to records and facilities
Applicable jurisdiction for regulatory purposes
Incident notification period to the contracting party
Data portability upon termination

III_3.titulo: Contingency Plan for Foreign Provider Replacement
III_3.descricao: Documented action plan for replacing the foreign provider in a crisis scenario, including identified alternatives and transition timelines.
III_3.justificativa_legal: Clause IV of IN 701 (continuity) combined with art. 83: dependence on a foreign provider is a continuity risk. A replacement plan is an implicit resilience requirement.
conteudo_minimo: Identified alternative providers (minimum 1)
Estimated transition timeline (RTO for replacement)
Plan activation triggers
Responsible party and approval

III_4.titulo: Annual Reassessment Report of Foreign Providers
III_4.descricao: Annual reassessment report for each foreign provider, confirming maintained capacities and regulatory compliance.
III_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 83 combined with art. 33: continuous monitoring. An annually documented reassessment demonstrates that control is systematic and not just at initial contracting.
conteudo_minimo: Current regulatory status in the home jurisdiction
SLA performance for the period
Material changes in the provider's structure or capacity
Decision: maintain or activate replacement plan

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP limits dependence on foreign providers to the strictly necessary, maintains two qualified alternative providers for each critical foreign service, conducts annual in-person or remote due diligence with a technical interview, and has BCB access clauses validated by internal legal counsel.
indicadores: Zero foreign providers without BCB access clause in the contract
Annual in-person or remote due diligence documented
Tested alternatives for high-criticality providers
Annual reassessment report per provider

IV.resumo: Documented and tested plans for recovering client VA positions and controls in the event of an incident at the VASP itself or a contracted provider.

IV_1.descricao: High-level policy approved by the board defining the business continuity programme, critical systems, RTO/RPO and testing cycle.
IV_1.justificativa_legal: Art. 34 and art. 43, IV of Res. 520: the VASP must document continuity plans with defined RTO/RPO. Without a formal policy there is no basis for operational plans.
conteudo_minimo: Definition of critical services and systems
RTO (Recovery Time Objective) and RPO (Recovery Point Objective) per service
Minimum testing cycle (annual) and scenarios covered
Continuity programme owner
Board approval with effective date and version control

IV_2.descricao: Recovery plan per critical system (ledger, custody, client APIs, payment systems) with detailed restoration steps.
IV_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 34 requires plans for recovering client VA positions. The DRP is the operational instrument enabling technical recovery within the defined RTO/RPO.
conteudo_minimo: Systems covered and recovery priority
Recovery steps with responsible parties
Backup sources and RTO/RPO per system
Internal and BCB communication during incident
Result of last documented test

IV_3.titulo: Result of Last Continuity Exercise (BCP/DR Test)
IV_3.descricao: Report of the last continuity exercise conducted, with scenarios tested, results, measured recovery times and improvement plan.
IV_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 43, §4 of Res. 520: continuity plans must be tested and documented with a minimum annual frequency. Without test evidence, the plan is theoretical only.
conteudo_minimo: Date and scenario of the exercise
Systems and processes tested
RTO/RPO measured vs. defined objective
Findings, identified gaps and remediation plan
Approval by the board or committee

IV_4.titulo: Contingency Plan for Critical Provider Failure
IV_4.descricao: Contingency procedure for unavailability of the most critical providers (cloud, custodian, payment processor), with alternatives and timelines.
IV_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 34: plans cover incidents at 'the VASP itself or a contracted provider'. Failure of a critical provider is a scenario explicitly covered by IN 701.
conteudo_minimo: Providers covered by the plan (minimum top 3 critical)
Replacement alternatives or degraded operation
Internal SLA for plan activation
Responsible party and client communication

IV_5.titulo: Client Communication Protocol During a Continuity Incident
IV_5.descricao: Procedure defining communication triggers, channels, minimum content and timelines in the event of a continuity incident.
IV_5.justificativa_legal: Art. 43, VII: client service and communication during incidents. Clause IV specifies recovery of client VA positions — proactive communication protects the client and demonstrates regulatory good faith.
conteudo_minimo: Communication triggers (incident type and severity)
Maximum time for first client communication
Primary and backup channel
Minimum communication content (what happened, impact, estimated timeline)

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP conducts full failover exercises semi-annually with RTO metrics under 4h for critical custody systems. Maintains hot-standby in a geographically separate secondary data centre, with synchronous ledger replication. The client communication plan is activated within 15 minutes after incident confirmation.
indicadores: RTO < 4h for critical custody systems
Full failover exercise conducted semi-annually
Active secondary data centre with synchronous replication
Client notification within 15 min after incident confirmation
5-year test history documented

V.resumo: Complete set of approved and maintained governance policies covering risk, compliance, IT, continuity, outsourcing and client service.

V_1.descricao: Portfolio of formal policies covering art. 43 topics: governance, operational risk, internal controls, continuity, compliance, IT/cybersecurity, client service and outsourcing.
V_1.justificativa_legal: Art. 43 caput and §1: policies must be prepared, approved by the board, periodically reviewed and communicated. The 'set' is explicit — a single generic policy does not satisfy.
conteudo_minimo: Corporate governance policy (administration structure, powers, mandates)
Operational risk management policy
Internal controls and segregation of duties policy
Business continuity policy
Compliance policy (AML/CFT, data protection)
Information / cybersecurity policy
Client service policy
Outsourcing / vendors policy
For each policy: approval date, owner, next review

V_2.titulo: Organisational Chart with Reporting Lines and Mandates
V_2.descricao: Updated organisational chart showing the administration structure, reporting lines, segregation between operations, risk and compliance, and designated owners.
V_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 43, I: the structure of responsibilities, powers and mandates must be documented. The org chart is the basic instrument for demonstrating governance and segregation of duties.
conteudo_minimo: Board / directors and members
Business and control units (risk, compliance, internal audit)
Reporting lines: independence of control functions
Formal nomination of compliance and risk owners

V_3.titulo: Training Programme and Policy Dissemination Minutes
V_3.descricao: Schedule and records of training conducted on corporate policies (attendance, date, content), and dissemination minutes for new employees.
V_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 43, §1: policies must be 'communicated and trained with responsible employees'. Without training evidence, the policy exists only on paper.
conteudo_minimo: Annual training programme with dates and contents
Attendance list per training session
Onboarding training for new employees
Knowledge assessment or comprehension test

V_4.descricao: Record of each policy's review dates over the past 24 months and upcoming planned reviews, with responsible party for each.
V_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 43, §1: reviewed 'at least annually or upon material change'. The calendar proves that review is systematic, not ad hoc.
conteudo_minimo: Policy name, date of last review, next review
Review result: unchanged, updated or replaced
Board approval of updated versions
Version history

V_5.descricao: Formal document (board minutes or resolution) designating the compliance officer, defining their responsibilities and ensuring independence.
V_5.justificativa_legal: Art. 43, §2: the VASP must designate a person responsible for policy compliance with 'adequate authority and independence'. This requirement is literal and verifiable.
conteudo_minimo: Name and title of the designated officer
Responsibilities and powers
Reporting line (preferably to board/CEO, not to business unit)
Designation date and tenure

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP has a Chief Compliance Officer (CCO) with a seat on the risk committee and a direct reporting line to the board, without subordination to the business unit. The 8 art. 43 policies are reviewed semi-annually, training is mandatory quarterly and assessed with a knowledge test. Every employee signs a policy acknowledgement form during onboarding.
indicadores: CCO with direct reporting line to the board
8 art. 43 policies reviewed semi-annually
100% of employees trained annually with test
Digital policy repository accessible 24/7 for BCB supervision
Review calendar published internally

VI_a.tema: AML/CFT — Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorism Financing
VI_a.resumo: Processes, policies and controls for compliance with AML/CFT legislation, including KYC, transaction monitoring, blockchain KYT and reporting to COAF.

VI_a_1.descricao: Formal policy approved by the board covering the entire AML/CFT programme: KYC, monitoring, COAF reporting and governance.
VI_a_1.justificativa_legal: AML/CFT legislation (Law 9.613 and BCB Circular 3.978) and art. 60 of Res. 520 require a formal AML programme. Without an approved policy there is no programme — this is an independent violation.
conteudo_minimo: Internal risk assessment (NRA — National Risk Assessment applied to the VASP)
KYC/KYA process (Know Your Customer / Know Your Asset)
Transaction and blockchain monitoring (KYT)
COAF / SISCOAF reporting process
Record retention (minimum 5 years — art. 45, II)
Programme owner and AML committee

VI_a_2.titulo: Client and Product Risk Assessment Matrix
VI_a_2.descricao: Methodology and result of risk assessment by client profile and product/service offered, with classification (low, medium, high) and associated controls.
VI_a_2.justificativa_legal: BCB Circular 3.978 and art. 60 of Res. 520: the risk-based approach requires documented risk assessment per client and product. This is the foundation of the AML programme.
conteudo_minimo: Client risk factors (volume, location, PEP, source of funds)
Product risk factors (volatile VA, staking, OTC, P2P)
Resulting risk classification per segment
Mitigating controls per risk level

VI_a_3.titulo: KYC/KYA Procedure with Sources and Responsible Parties
VI_a_3.descricao: Step-by-step client onboarding process: required documentation, verification sources, approval decision, continuous monitoring and data updates.
VI_a_3.justificativa_legal: BCB Circular 3.978: client identification and qualification are fundamental obligations. Without a documented procedure, KYC is arbitrary and not auditable.
conteudo_minimo: Documents required by client type (individual, corporate, international)
Verification sources (public databases, credit bureaus, sanctions lists)
Enhanced Due Diligence process for high risk
Data update frequency by risk profile
Criteria and responsible party for approval / rejection

VI_a_4.descricao: Technical description of the KYT tool in use (Chainalysis, Elliptic, TRM Labs or equivalent), coverage scope and evidence of configured alerts.
VI_a_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 60, II of Res. 520 / clause VIII of IN 701: blockchain monitoring is part of the required continuous monitoring. For VASPs, KYT is the primary frontier for detecting on-chain laundering.
conteudo_minimo: Tool name and blockchain and token coverage
Configured alert rules (sanctioned addresses, darknet, ransomware)
Alert treatment process (SLA, responsible, escalation)
Transaction volume monitored in the period
Sample of treated alerts (anonymised)
VI_a_4.certik_nota: SkyInsights provides real-time transaction monitoring and on-chain analysis (300M+ address labels). Covers the blockchain layer of market surveillance.

VI_a_5.descricao: Records of AML training conducted in the last 12 months: date, content, participants and assessment.
VI_a_5.justificativa_legal: BCB Circular 3.978: periodic training is mandatory in the AML programme. Without evidence, the BCB cannot confirm that employees have the capacity to operate the controls.
conteudo_minimo: Mandatory annual training for all employees
Specific training for KYC and compliance analysts
Learning assessment (test or certificate)
Update training when relevant legislation changes

VI_a_6.titulo: Sample Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) to COAF
VI_a_6.descricao: Anonymised SAR sample (without client identification) demonstrating that the COAF reporting process exists in production. May be replaced by a formal declaration if no SAR was necessary in the period.
VI_a_6.justificativa_legal: Law 9.613 and BCB Circular 3.978: reporting suspicious transactions to COAF is a legal obligation. A sample (anonymised) proves the process exists in production.
conteudo_minimo: Date and generic reason for reporting (without personal data)
Decision to report (who, when, regulatory basis)
Or: formal declaration that no SAR occurred in the period with justification

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP operates with 100% real-time on-chain transaction screening via enterprise-level KYT, has dedicated compliance analysts with an alert queue managed under a 4h SLA, conducts an annual NRA and submitted the last SAR to COAF within 24h of the decision trigger. Training is certified and the programme is reviewed by an independent external auditor annually.
indicadores: 100% of transactions screened in real time via KYT
Alert treatment SLA: 4h for high risk, 24h for medium
NRA documented and updated annually
Programme reviewed by external auditor annually
Zero delayed COAF reporting (within legal deadline)

VI_b.resumo: Demonstrate that the computational environment (infrastructure, systems, networks and cloud) meets the minimum security, resilience and operation requirements of art. 48 of Res. 520.

VI_b_1.descricao: Formal approved information security policy covering access management, data classification, encryption, vulnerability management and incident response.
VI_b_1.justificativa_legal: Art. 48, I to VIII of Res. 520 requires specific computational security controls. The policy is the instrument that organises and institutionally legitimises them.
conteudo_minimo: Data and system classification (critical, sensitive, public)
Least-privilege access control (art. 48, II)
Identity and authentication management (mandatory MFA)
Encryption in transit and at rest
Vulnerability and patch management
Event monitoring and logging
Incident response (aligned with clause XII of IN 701)

VI_b_2.descricao: Review report for privileged access to critical systems (ledger, custody, production servers, cloud consoles), demonstrating only authorised persons have access.
VI_b_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 48, II of Res. 520: access control is an explicit requirement. Periodic review documents that the control is operationalised and not merely defined in the policy.
conteudo_minimo: Systems reviewed and review date
Number of privileged accounts before and after review
Removed or deactivated accounts and reason
Review and approval responsible party
Frequency: minimum quarterly

VI_b_3.descricao: Dashboard or report on vulnerability management showing the scanning, prioritisation and patch application process by criticality.
VI_b_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 48, IV and V of Res. 520: vulnerability detection and remediation are components of a secure environment. Without a report, the control is invisible to the auditor.
conteudo_minimo: Scanning tools used and system scope
Open vulnerabilities by criticality (CVSS)
Patching SLA by criticality level
Critical vulnerabilities: maximum 72h deadline
Patching SLA compliance history

VI_b_4.descricao: Screenshot or report from the SIEM/monitoring tool demonstrating critical system coverage, configured alerts and recent event log.
VI_b_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 48, IV: critical systems must be monitored. Evidence of the SIEM in operation (dashboard, configuration or log review sample) proves that monitoring is real.
conteudo_minimo: Systems covered by monitoring
Types of configured alerts (authentication, privileged access, anomaly)
Volume of events processed in the period
Sample of treated alert with resolution

VI_b_5.titulo: Incident Response Plan and Exercise Evidence
VI_b_5.descricao: Security incident response plan (IRP) with defined escalation, communication and roles, complemented by evidence of a tabletop or simulation exercise conducted.
VI_b_5.justificativa_legal: Art. 48, VI of Res. 520 and clause XII of IN 701: an incident response plan is an explicit requirement. A documented exercise proves operationalisation.
conteudo_minimo: Incident classification by severity
Triage, containment, eradication and recovery process
Roles and responsibilities (RACI)
BCB and client communication per clause XII
Last exercise result: scenario, participants, response time

VI_b_6.titulo: Cloud Provider SOC 2 or ISO 27001 Report
VI_b_6.descricao: SOC 2 Type II report or ISO 27001 certificate from the main cloud provider used, demonstrating base infrastructure meets recognised standards.
VI_b_6.justificativa_legal: Art. 48 combined with art. 32 of Res. 520: computational environment security includes cloud infrastructure. The provider's certificate is trusted third-party evidence.
conteudo_minimo: SOC 2 or ISO 27001 report period covered
Certification scope (services/data centres relevant to the VASP)
Relevant findings and auditor opinions
Certificate expiry date

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP operates in an ISO 27001 certified environment, with an enterprise-level SIEM covering 100% of critical systems, automated weekly privileged access review, critical patching within 24h and a quarterly incident response exercise. The cloud environment has an updated SOC 2 Type II report and the VASP has its own ISO 27001 certification.
indicadores: Own ISO 27001 or in progress
SIEM covering 100% of critical systems
Critical patches (CVSS ≥ 9) within 24h
Automated weekly privileged access review
Quarterly documented tabletop exercise

VII.tema: Key Management and VA Control Instrument Policies
VII.resumo: Demonstrate robust policies for generating, storing, accessing, rotating and destroying private keys or other VA control instruments for client virtual assets.
VII.gatilho: Mandatory when the VASP directly or through a sub-custodian holds private keys for clients.

VII_1.descricao: Formal policy defining the entire key lifecycle management: architecture (cold/warm/hot/MPC), generation, storage, access, rotation and secure destruction.
VII_1.justificativa_legal: Arts. 77–80 of Res. 520 and clause VII of IN 701: the VASP must have formal policies for custody and protection of control instruments. Without an approved policy, any custody practice is undocumented and unauditable.
conteudo_minimo: Custody architecture: cold, warm, hot wallet, MPC, multisig — with justification
Maximum percentage of VA in hot wallet
Key generation process (ceremony) with minimum quorum
Access control to keys: strong authentication, least privilege principle
Rotation process and maximum time without rotation
Secure destruction process for old keys

VII_2.descricao: Record of the last key generation ceremony: date, location (air-gapped or HSM environment), participants (with roles and witnesses), procedures followed and result.
VII_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 77 of Res. 520: key generation must follow secure procedures with traceability. The documented ceremony is evidence that the process was controlled from the origin.
conteudo_minimo: Date, location and security environment (air-gap, HSM model)
Participants with defined roles (quorum)
Step-by-step procedure followed
Integrity verification of generated keys
Participant signatures

VII_3.descricao: Diagram showing the complete custody architecture: cold, warm and hot storage environments; HSMs used; access controls; segregation between client and own keys.
VII_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 76, V: the custodian VASP must maintain control structure over VA. The diagram demonstrates the architecture to the auditor clearly and verifiably.
conteudo_minimo: Cold, warm and hot storage environments identified
HSMs (brand, model) and data centre locations
Access controls per environment
Fund movement flow between environments (with required quorum)
Separation of client vs. operational keys

VII_4.titulo: Key Rotation Procedure and Last Rotation Evidence
VII_4.descricao: Documented key rotation procedure and record of the last rotation performed, with date, scope and result.
VII_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 78 of Res. 520: the VASP must keep protection of control instruments up to date. Periodic rotation is a fundamental key security control.
conteudo_minimo: Rotation frequency by key type (operational, master, backup)
Rotation process without service interruption
Post-rotation verification
Last rotation log with date and responsible party

VII_5.descricao: Audit or independent assessment report of key custody controls conducted in the last 24 months by a specialised firm.
VII_5.justificativa_legal: Art. 79 of Res. 520 and clause VII of IN 701: independent review of custody controls is a maturity indicator and proof of effectiveness. For a custodian VASP, this is the highest-value probative document.
conteudo_minimo: Scope: systems, processes and controls assessed
Audit methodology used
Main findings (classified by severity)
Remediation plan and implementation status
VII_5.certik_nota: CertiK provides DLT Security Solutions including institutional architecture design and security assessments for custody systems involving smart contracts or on-chain components.

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP uses enterprise-level MPC (Multi-Party Computation) with FIPS 140-3 Level 3 certified HSMs, maximum 2% of VA in hot wallet, generation ceremony with a 5-person quorum in an air-gapped room, and annual independent review by a firm specialised in crypto custody. Operational key rotation is automatic every 90 days.
indicadores: MPC or multisig with minimum 3-of-5 quorum
FIPS 140-3 Level 3 certified HSMs
Max. 2% of VA in hot wallet
Documented generation ceremony in air-gapped environment
Annual independent custody audit
Automatic operational key rotation every 90 days

VIII.tema: Continuous Security Monitoring and Risk Management
VIII.resumo: Operational mechanisms for continuous security monitoring, operational risk assessment, vulnerability management and incident detection/response.

VIII_1.descricao: Technical document describing the monitoring tool (SIEM, SOC or equivalent), coverage scope, configured alert types and responsible team.
VIII_1.justificativa_legal: Clause VIII requires demonstrating continuous monitoring mechanisms. The technical description is the primary evidence that the monitoring infrastructure exists and operates.
conteudo_minimo: Tool name and licensing model
Critical systems covered (ledger, APIs, custody, administration)
Types of configured alerts and thresholds
Coverage hours (8x5, 24x7)
Responsible team (internal or outsourced SOC)

VIII_2.descricao: Updated operational risk map (anonymised version for audit), with identification, probability/impact assessment, mitigating controls and action plans.
VIII_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 43, II of Res. 520: identification, assessment, monitoring and mitigation of risks. The risk map is the central instrument organising the entire risk management programme.
conteudo_minimo: Identified risks by category (technological, operational, custody, market)
Assessment: probability × impact (before and after controls)
Mitigating controls per risk
Risk owner and remediation deadline for open risks
Last update date (minimum quarterly)

VIII_3.descricao: Executive summary of the last external penetration test report conducted by an independent firm, covering the VASP's public surfaces.
VIII_3.justificativa_legal: Clause VIII: risk assessment and vulnerability detection. External pentest is the most objective assessment of resistance to real attacks. Without it, monitoring is defensive but not proactive.
conteudo_minimo: Executing firm and methodology (PTES, OWASP, TIBER-EU)
Scope: APIs, client portal, admin panel
Findings classified by criticality (Critical/High/Medium/Low)
Remediation plan with deadlines and implementation status
Date of last execution: maximum 12 months validity
VIII_3.certik_nota: CertiK Penetration Testing covers APIs, client portal, admin panels and Web3 infrastructure — 500+ applications secured, 5,000+ APIs hardened.

VIII_4.titulo: KRIs — Key Risk Indicators with History
VIII_4.descricao: Set of KRIs (Key Risk Indicators) monitored, with defined alert and action thresholds, and 12-month evolution history.
VIII_4.justificativa_legal: Clause VIII combined with clause XI: continuous management requires measurable indicators. KRIs prove that risk management is quantitative and not merely qualitative.
conteudo_minimo: List of KRIs by risk category
Alert and action threshold for each KRI
12-month reading history
Breaches that occurred and responses taken
Senior management reporting frequency

VIII_5.titulo: Risk Management Report for Senior Management
VIII_5.descricao: Quarterly risk management report presented to the board or risk committee, summarising the risk map, KRIs and incidents for the period.
VIII_5.justificativa_legal: Art. 43, I and II of Res. 520: senior management must be informed about the risk profile. Documented quarterly reporting proves that risk governance functions systematically.
conteudo_minimo: Executive summary of the period's risk profile
KRIs with quarterly variation
Relevant incidents and remediation status
Emerging risks identified
Minutes or evidence of presentation to senior management

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP has an enterprise-level SIEM (Splunk, Microsoft Sentinel or equivalent) with 24x7 coverage by a dedicated SOC, semi-annual external pentest by a CREST-certified firm, risk map automatically updated with monitoring system data, and monthly risk report to the board. Critical KRIs trigger automatic alerts to the CEO.
indicadores: 24x7 SIEM with dedicated or managed SOC
Semi-annual external pentest by CREST-certified firm
Risk map updated monthly
Automatic KRIs with CEO alerts for critical breaches
Monthly risk report to the board
MTTD (Mean Time to Detect) < 1h for critical incidents

IX_a.tema: Virtual Asset Listing, Suspension and Delisting
IX_a.resumo: Formal policies governing the decision to offer, suspend or remove a virtual asset from the platform, with objective criteria and client communication process.
IX_a.gatilho: Triggered when the VASP lists or intends to list VA for trading or custody.

IX_a_1.descricao: Formal policy defining new VA admission criteria, continuous monitoring of listed assets and the suspension and delisting process.
IX_a_1.justificativa_legal: Art. 64 of Res. 520 and clause IX(a) of IN 701: requires formal policies for offering/listing/suspending/delisting. Without a policy, decisions are arbitrary and unauditable.
conteudo_minimo: Admission criteria (technical, legal, liquidity, market risk, compliance)
Approval process (committee, quorum, frequency)
Temporary suspension criteria and activation triggers
Delisting criteria and minimum client notice period
Periodic review of listed VA (minimum frequency)
Policy made available to the client (art. 64, §1)

IX_a_2.titulo: New Asset Assessment Checklist (Listing Due Diligence)
IX_a_2.descricao: Template and examples of assessment checklist used when admitting a new VA, covering technical, legal, risk and compliance dimensions.
IX_a_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 64: selection must follow criteria established in the policy. The assessment checklist is evidence that the policy is applied systematically, not subjectively case by case.
conteudo_minimo: Technical analysis (protocol, smart contract, decentralisation)
Legal analysis (regulatory classification, jurisdiction restrictions)
Market risk analysis (liquidity, volatility, market cap)
Compliance analysis (AML history, use in illicit activities)
IX_a_2.certik_nota: CertiK audits the smart contract of the candidate token, providing a report that feeds the listing checklist with objective independent technical evidence.

IX_a_3.titulo: Listing Committee Minutes or Decision Record
IX_a_3.descricao: Minutes or decision record of the listing committee (anonymised if necessary), demonstrating analysis, discussion and formal approval of recent listings.
IX_a_3.justificativa_legal: The listing decision is formal and must be documented. The committee minutes prove that the governance process was followed and the decision was collegial.
conteudo_minimo: Meeting date and members present
VA(s) assessed and assessment result (approved/rejected)
Decision rationale
Participant signatures or digital record

IX_a_4.titulo: Sample Client Communication on Suspension or Delisting
IX_a_4.descricao: Template or anonymised example of client communication sent in the event of a VA suspension or delisting.
IX_a_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 64, §1: the policy must be available to the client. Communication about suspension/delisting events proves that the transparency process functions in production.
conteudo_minimo: Notice period before effective date (minimum per policy)
Communication channel used
Content: reason, client impact, deadline and alternatives
Process for client withdrawal of delisted VA

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP operates with an independent listing committee (no commercial unit voting participation), quarterly review of all listed VA with automatic delisting if criteria are not maintained, and makes the listing policy publicly available on the main page before login.
indicadores: Listing committee with independent members
Quarterly review of all listed VA
Listing policy publicly available before login
Documented due diligence for each listed VA
Client notice minimum 30 days before delisting

IX_b.resumo: Specific criteria for selecting and monitoring fiat-referenced VA (stablecoins), including issuer verification, reserves and peg monitoring.
IX_b.gatilho: Triggered when the VASP offers or intends to offer stablecoins or fiat-referenced VA.

IX_b_1.descricao: Specific policy for selecting stablecoins and fiat-referenced VA, covering the art. 65, I to IX criteria of Res. 520.
IX_b_1.justificativa_legal: Art. 65 of Res. 520 lists 9 specific selection criteria for fiat-referenced VA. The policy must incorporate these criteria in an operationalisable way.
conteudo_minimo: Selection criteria aligned with art. 65, I to IX
Issuer reserve verification (type, coverage, attestation)
Peg monitoring (alerts and deviation limits)
Specific risk disclosure to the client (depeg, centralised issuer)
Suspension process in the event of peg deviation

IX_b_2.descricao: Document per stablecoin or fiat-referenced VA listed, with issuer characteristics, reserve structure, audit history and identified risks.
IX_b_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 65, I: verification of the referenced VA's characteristics. The technical sheet documents the diligence performed on each stablecoin and serves as a reference for continuous monitoring.
conteudo_minimo: Issuer and registration jurisdiction
Reserve structure (type of assets, declared coverage)
Most recent reserve attestation report (link or copy)
History of peg deviation events
Identified risks and mitigating controls
IX_b_2.certik_nota: CertiK provides smart contract audits of the stablecoin and Skynet profile with security analysis, feeding the technical sheet with independently verified data.

IX_b_3.descricao: Dashboard or alert configuration demonstrating real-time peg monitoring of offered stablecoins, with alert thresholds and event history.
IX_b_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 65, V and IX: the VASP must monitor relevant events that may affect the referenced VA. Peg monitoring is the most direct operational control for stablecoins.
conteudo_minimo: Stablecoins monitored and price source used
Alert threshold (e.g.: deviation > 0.5%) and action threshold (e.g.: > 2%)
History of alerts triggered in the period
Decision process upon alert activation

IX_b_4.descricao: Term or section of contract/terms of use disclosing stablecoin-specific risks to the client before the first operation.
IX_b_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 65, VIII of Res. 520 and par1_I: risk disclosure to the client. For stablecoins, risks are specific (depeg, issuer failure, reserve risk) and must be communicated separately.
conteudo_minimo: Depeg risk (loss of parity with the reference fiat)
Issuer risk (centralised, no FGC coverage)
Reserve risk (reserve assets may lose value)
Impact in case of issuer insolvency

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP only offers regulated stablecoins (or with quarterly Big 4 attestation), monitors peg in real time with automatic suspension upon 2% deviation, and discloses the risk sheet for each stablecoin on the product page before the client's first operation.
indicadores: Only stablecoins with quarterly reserve attestation
Automatic suspension at 2% peg deviation
Risk sheet per stablecoin publicly available
Semi-annual review of all listed referenced VA

X_a.tema: Internal Controls — Conflicts of Interest and Segregation of Duties
X_a.resumo: Policies and controls to identify, disclose and manage conflicts of interest between the VASP, its officers and clients, with effective duty segregation.
X_a.gatilho: Fixed mandatory on the intermediary track; mandatory on all tracks.

X_a_1.descricao: Formal policy covering the 9 clauses of art. 69: identification, disclosure, management and prevention of conflicts of interest between the VASP, officers and clients.
X_a_1.justificativa_legal: Art. 69 of Res. 520 lists 9 conflict situations the VASP must identify, disclose and manage. Without a formal policy it is impossible to demonstrate that these 9 requirements are systematically covered.
conteudo_minimo: Definition of conflict of interest applied to the VASP
Map of the 9 clauses of art. 69 and associated controls
Conflict identification and disclosure process
Decision to disclose to client vs. abstain from operation
Conflicts committee and responsible party
Annual review or upon material event

X_a_2.titulo: Functional Segregation Map (Org Chart + Reporting Lines)
X_a_2.descricao: Detailed organisational chart showing segregation between incompatible functions (own trading vs. client order management, custody vs. operations, compliance vs. commercial).
X_a_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 69 and art. 43, III of Res. 520: segregation of duties is a fundamental control. The map evidences to the auditor that incompatible functions are not performed by the same person or unit.
conteudo_minimo: Identified incompatible functions (own trading vs. client)
Independent reporting lines for risk and compliance
Compensating controls where full segregation is not feasible
Review of segregation upon organisational changes

X_a_3.titulo: Records of Identified and Treated Conflicts
X_a_3.descricao: Log or register of conflicts of interest identified in the last 12 months, with decision taken (disclosure, abstention or alternative treatment) and responsible party.
X_a_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 69: conflicts must be identified and treated. A register demonstrates that the process is operational and not merely declaratory. Without records, there is no proof that the policy is applied.
conteudo_minimo: Date and type of conflict identified
Decision: disclose to client, abstain or alternative control
Decision responsible party
Result and closure

X_a_4.descricao: Procedure defining when and how conflicts of interest are disclosed to the client, including format, deadline and communication channel.
X_a_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 69, IV of Res. 520: the VASP must disclose to the client conflicts it cannot eliminate. The procedure ensures this disclosure is systematic and documented.
conteudo_minimo: Disclosure trigger (before the operation or upon conflict identification)
Disclosure format (email, terms of use, system popup)
Minimum disclosure content
Disclosure record and client consent

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP has a total barrier between the proprietary trading desk and client order management, with an automated Chinese Wall system, a conflicts committee that meets monthly, and proactive disclosure of any conflict to the client before executing the operation. Conflict records are internally audited semi-annually.
indicadores: Automated and systematically audited Chinese Wall
Conflicts committee with documented monthly meetings
Zero operations executed with undisclosed conflict
Semi-annual internal audit of conflict records
Annual mandatory conflicts of interest training

XI.tema: Risk, Capital, Compliance and Internal Audit Management
XI.resumo: Risk and capital management structures, compliance function and internal audit operationalised in accordance with BCB/authorised institution regulations.

XI_1.titulo: Three Lines of Defence Organisational Chart
XI_1.descricao: Organisational chart demonstrating the 3 lines of defence: 1st (business/operations), 2nd (risk and compliance), 3rd (internal audit), with independent reporting lines.
XI_1.justificativa_legal: Arts. 55–58 of Res. 520 require risk, compliance and audit structures aligned with financial institution regulation. The Three Lines of Defence model is the BCB-accepted regulatory standard.
conteudo_minimo: 1st line: business units and own control responsibilities
2nd line: risk and compliance independent from business
3rd line: internal audit reporting to board/audit committee
Reporting lines for each line (no reporting to business unit)

XI_2.descricao: Risk management policy covering operational, market, liquidity and custody risk, with assessment methodology, risk limits and supporting capital structure.
XI_2.justificativa_legal: Arts. 55 and 56 of Res. 520: the VASP must have a formalised risk management structure. Without an approved policy there is no basis for identification, assessment and mitigation activities.
conteudo_minimo: Risk categories covered and assessment methodology
Risk appetite and limits approved by the board
Minimum capital structure for operational loss absorption
Board escalation upon reaching risk limits
Review cycle (minimum annual)

XI_3.descricao: Periodic report (semi-annual or annual) from the compliance function, assessing the VASP's adherence to regulatory obligations, with status per obligation and identified gaps.
XI_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 57 of Res. 520: the compliance function must report systematically. The report is evidence that the 2nd line is active and operating independently.
conteudo_minimo: Map of applicable regulatory obligations
Compliance status per obligation
Identified gaps and remediation plans with deadlines
Identified emerging regulatory risks
Recommendations and open actions

XI_4.titulo: Internal Audit Plan and Last Cycle Report
XI_4.descricao: Annual internal audit plan and executive summary of the last audit cycle report, with main findings and action plan.
XI_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 58 of Res. 520: internal audit must assess control effectiveness. The plan demonstrates adequate coverage; the report demonstrates audits are actually conducted.
conteudo_minimo: Plan: areas audited, schedule and methodology
Report: findings by severity (critical, high, medium)
Action plan: responsible party, deadline and implementation status
Internal auditor opinion on control effectiveness
Evidence of presentation to board / audit committee

XI_5.descricao: Tracker or follow-up report on recommendations from the last internal audits, with updated status for each item.
XI_5.justificativa_legal: Internal audit only creates value if its recommendations are implemented. Documented follow-up demonstrates maturity of the control cycle.
conteudo_minimo: Recommendation, responsible area and agreed deadline
Status: open, in progress, completed
Closure evidence for completed items
Reopened findings or past-deadline items — escalation

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP has a dedicated internal audit team (or outsourced to a recognised firm), reporting to the board's audit committee, with a risk-based audit plan covering 100% of critical processes in 18-month cycles. The compliance report is presented to the board monthly with regulatory KPI status.
indicadores: Internal audit reporting directly to the board
100% of critical processes audited in 18-month cycle
Monthly compliance report to the board
Recommendation implementation rate > 90% within deadline
Audit committee with an independent external member

XII.tema: Cybersecurity Policy and Incident Response Plan
XII.resumo: Formal cybersecurity policy and incident action and response plan in accordance with applicable regulations for BCB-authorised institutions.

XII_1.descricao: Formal cybersecurity policy approved and published, covering objectives, principles, controls and maintenance cycle per BCB Resolution 85/2021.
XII_1.justificativa_legal: Clause XII of IN 701 requires a cybersecurity policy in accordance with applicable regulation (BCB Res. 85/2021). Without this document the VASP is in literal violation of IN 701.
conteudo_minimo: Cybersecurity objectives and scope
Identification of critical assets and sensitive data
Security controls by category (access, network, endpoint, data)
Cyber incident management
Security training and awareness
Board approval, effective date and next review

XII_2.titulo: Cyber Incident Action and Response Plan
XII_2.descricao: Detailed cyber incident response plan: classification, roles, triage process, containment, eradication, recovery and BCB communication.
XII_2.justificativa_legal: Clause XII and BCB Res. 85/2021: the action and response plan is literally required. It is distinct from the policy — it is the operational document guiding real-time response.
conteudo_minimo: Incident categories and severity classification criteria
RACI: roles and responsibilities in response
Triage, containment, eradication and recovery steps
BCB notification process and deadline (relevant incidents)
Client communication process
Incident declaration and closure criteria

XII_3.titulo: Result of Last Incident Response Exercise
XII_3.descricao: Report or minutes of the last incident response exercise (tabletop or simulation), with tested scenario, participants and results.
XII_3.justificativa_legal: BCB Res. 85/2021 and art. 43, §4 of Res. 520: plans must be tested. Without a documented exercise, the plan is theoretical and the BCB cannot trust in response capability.
conteudo_minimo: Date and scenario of the exercise (type of simulated incident)
Participants and roles
Response time measured vs. defined SLA
Identified gaps and improvement plan
Next planned exercise date

XII_4.titulo: Data Classification Policy and Controls by Sensitivity
XII_4.descricao: Data classification policy (public, internal, confidential, critical) and security controls defined per classification level.
XII_4.justificativa_legal: BCB Res. 85/2021 and LGPD: data must be classified and protected according to their sensitivity. Without classification, security controls are the same for everything — which is inadequate.
conteudo_minimo: Classification categories and inclusion criteria
Security controls per category (access, encryption, retention)
Classification process when creating or receiving data
Treatment of client data (aligned with LGPD)

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP has an ISO 27001 certified cybersecurity policy, incident response plan with a containment SLA < 2h for critical incidents, quarterly tabletop exercise, and automatic BCB notification configured in incident playbooks. All client data is encrypted with client-managed keys (BYOK) in a cloud environment.
indicadores: Own ISO 27001 or equivalent certification
Containment SLA < 2h for critical incidents
Quarterly tabletop exercise with report
Automatic BCB notification in incident playbooks
BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) for client data

XIII.resumo: Policies and mechanisms to identify, monitor and prevent wash trading, pump-and-dump, spoofing and other manipulative practices in the VASP's offered VA market.

XIII_1.titulo: Market Conduct and Spurious Practices Prevention Policy
XIII_1.descricao: Formal market conduct policy defining prohibited practices, surveillance mechanisms, investigation process and COAF communication when applicable.
XIII_1.justificativa_legal: Arts. 67–70 of Res. 520 and clause XIII of IN 701: the VASP must have formal policies and mechanisms. Without a policy, technical mechanisms have no institutional basis and are not auditable.
conteudo_minimo: Definition of prohibited spurious practices (wash trading, spoofing, pump-and-dump, front running)
Surveillance mechanisms implemented (tool, coverage, alerts)
Alert investigation process (SLA, responsible, escalation)
COAF communication criteria and process
Biennial independent review (art. 70, IV of Res. 520)

XIII_2.titulo: Technical Description of Market Surveillance Mechanisms
XIII_2.descricao: Technical documentation of the market surveillance tool or system: pair coverage, types of configured alerts and treatment process.
XIII_2.justificativa_legal: Clause XIII requires 'mechanisms' — not just a policy. The technical description proves that operational detection systems exist, not merely statements of intent.
conteudo_minimo: Tool used (internal or outsourced) and coverage
Types of patterns detected (wash trading, spoofing, etc.)
Configured alert thresholds by practice type
Integration with the platform's order management system
XIII_2.certik_nota: SkyInsights includes transaction monitoring and on-chain suspicious pattern detection (wash trading, high-risk addresses, volume anomalies). Covers the blockchain layer of market surveillance.

XIII_3.descricao: Anonymised sample of alerts generated by the surveillance system in the last 6 months, with decision taken in each case (closed, investigated, reported to COAF).
XIII_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 70 of Res. 520: mechanisms must be operational. A sample of treated alerts demonstrates the system functions in production and is not merely configured.
conteudo_minimo: Alert type and date (without client identification)
Investigation result: false positive or confirmed suspicion
Decision and rationale
COAF communication (if applicable)

XIII_4.titulo: Biennial Independent Review Report of the Surveillance Programme
XIII_4.descricao: Independent review report of the spurious practices surveillance programme, conducted every 2 years by a specialised firm.
XIII_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 70, IV of Res. 520: biennial independent review is an explicit requirement. Without this document, the VASP is in literal violation of art. 70, IV.
conteudo_minimo: Reviewing firm and review scope
Methodology applied
Findings on mechanism effectiveness
Recommendations and implementation plan
XIII_4.certik_nota: CertiK can perform independent security and compliance reviews as a recognised third-party auditor, delivering a formal report aligned with the art. 70, IV requirement.

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP operates with an enterprise-level market surveillance system (NICE Actimize, Eventus Systems or equivalent), covering 100% of orders and transactions in real time, with annual independent review (more frequent than the required biennial minimum) and dedicated analysts with a 2h SLA alert queue.
indicadores: 100% of orders and transactions monitored in real time
Alert treatment SLA < 2h for high priority
Annual independent review (above biennial minimum)
Zero detected wash trading unreported to COAF

XIV.resumo: Formal VA custody contract with clients, containing the minimum elements of arts. 73, 75 and 78 of Res. 520, including obligations, rights, wind-down plan and incident notification.
XIV.gatilho: Mandatory when the VASP custodies client VA, directly or through a sub-custodian.

XIV_1.descricao: Current VA custody contract template, with the minimum elements of arts. 73, 75 and 78, reviewed by legal counsel.
XIV_1.justificativa_legal: Clause XIV of IN 701 and art. 73 of Res. 520: the custody contract must contain the minimum elements of art. 73, I to X. Without a formal contract, custody has no adequate legal basis.
conteudo_minimo: Identification of parties and VA covered
Custodian obligations (arts. 73 and 75)
Client rights and responsibilities
Termination and VA return process (art. 78, III)
Client incident notification (art. 73, IX)
Sub-custody: conditions and responsibilities (art. 73, VIII)
Fee and charge disclosure

XIV_2.descricao: Policy defining sub-custodian selection criteria, responsibilities, monitoring and sub-custodian obligations in relation to the client contract.
XIV_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 73, VIII of Res. 520: sub-custody is regulated and requires consent and responsibility. Without a procedure, the VASP does not demonstrate it adequately controls the sub-custodian.
conteudo_minimo: Sub-custodian selection criteria
VASP's responsibilities to the client even when using a sub-custodian
Required clauses in the sub-custodian contract
Continuous sub-custodian monitoring
Client consent (when required)

XIV_3.descricao: Documented plan for the VASP's orderly wind-down or custody service termination, detailing the VA return process for each client.
XIV_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 78, III of Res. 520: the VASP must have a plan for orderly termination and VA return. Without this plan, a potential insolvency or voluntary closure would be disorderly and harmful to clients.
conteudo_minimo: Plan activation triggers (insolvency, voluntary closure, authorisation revocation)
Final VA inventory process per client
Return process (external wallets, timelines)
Client and BCB communication
Wind-down manager

XIV_4.descricao: Template or anonymised example of a custody incident notification sent to the client (unauthorised access, loss, unavailability).
XIV_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 73, IX of Res. 520: the contract must provide for client incident notification. Real evidence proves the process functions in production.
conteudo_minimo: Incident type (without sensitive data)
Notification deadline after incident confirmation
Minimum content: what occurred, client impact, measures taken
Communication channel used

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP has a custody contract reviewed annually by lawyers specialised in crypto assets and BCB regulation, wind-down plan tested in an annual exercise, and automatic client notification within 1h after confirmation of any custody incident. The contract includes a custody service availability SLA.
indicadores: Contract reviewed annually by specialised lawyers
Wind-down plan tested in annual exercise
Client notification within 1h after custody incident
Custody service SLA in the contract
100% of clients with signed contract before first operation

XV.tema: Custodian Measures and Plans (art. 76, I to XII)
XV.resumo: Set of 12 operational measures the custodian must implement: reconciliation, key security, insurance, reports, recovery and BCB communication.
XV.gatilho: Mandatory for VASPs with own custody (custodian and broker/exchange).

XV_1.descricao: Daily reconciliation procedure between the internal sub-ledger and on-chain positions, with record and discrepancy treatment.
XV_1.justificativa_legal: Art. 76, I of Res. 520: the custodian must maintain an updated inventory of VA. Daily reconciliation is the operational control that ensures the inventory is correct every day.
conteudo_minimo: Frequency: minimum daily for critical systems
Systems reconciled and data sources
Discrepancy treatment (alert, investigation, correction)
Responsible party and reconciliation result approval
Recent reconciliation sample (anonymised)

XV_2.descricao: See VII_1 — Private Key Management Policy. For clause XV, specific focus on art. 76, II and V requirements.
XV_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 76, II and V of Res. 520: key generation, storage and protection are explicit custodian requirements. The policy formalises the entire key lifecycle.
conteudo_minimo: Key generation in a secure environment (art. 76, II)
Storage in certified HSM or equivalent
Access control with strong authentication and quorum
Rotation and revocation (art. 76, V)

XV_3.titulo: Result of Last Asset/Key Recovery Test
XV_3.descricao: Report of the last VA and/or key recovery test, demonstrating the process works end-to-end in a simulation scenario.
XV_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 76, IX of Res. 520: the custodian must periodically test VA recovery capacity. Without test results, the recovery plan is merely theoretical.
conteudo_minimo: Scenario tested (HSM failure, primary key loss, etc.)
Result: successful recovery or gaps
Recovery time measured vs. defined RTO
Improvement plan for identified gaps

XV_4.descricao: Template or anonymised sample of the position report issued to the client, covering all fields required by art. 76, III.
XV_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 76, III of Res. 520 and par1_III of IN 701: the custodian must issue a position report to the client. The template demonstrates the process exists and content is adequate.
conteudo_minimo: VA held by type and quantity
Reference value (when available)
Reference date and time
Period movements (deposits and withdrawals)
Delivery method (portal, email, API)

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP performs automatic real-time reconciliation (not just daily), with immediate alerts for any discrepancy, and tests complete VA recovery quarterly. The position report is available to the client in real time via portal and API, with complete movement history.
indicadores: Automatic real-time reconciliation with immediate alerts
Quarterly documented VA recovery test
Real-time position report via portal and API
Zero discrepancies unaddressed for > 24h

XVI.resumo: Redundancy mechanisms for private keys and VA control instruments, ensuring that a single failure does not result in irreversible loss.
XVI.gatilho: Mandatory for VASPs with own or outsourced custody when responsible for redundancy.

XVI_1.descricao: Diagram and documentation of key redundancy architecture: copy locations (geographically distributed), redundancy type and test frequency.
XVI_1.justificativa_legal: Clause XVI and art. 76, XII of Res. 520: redundancy is an explicit requirement. Without documented architecture, it is impossible to demonstrate that redundancy exists and is adequate.
conteudo_minimo: Number of redundancy copies and location (different data centres)
Redundancy type: RAID, backup HSM, Shamir shares, etc.
Update frequency for redundancy copies
Access control to redundancy copies
Estimated failover time

XVI_2.descricao: Standard Operating Procedures for activating each redundancy scenario: primary HSM failure, site destruction, key corruption.
XVI_2.justificativa_legal: Clause XVII (recovery) complements clause XVI (redundancy). SOPs are the operational instrument ensuring redundancy can be activated in a controlled manner.
conteudo_minimo: Scenarios covered (minimum 3: HSM failure, site destruction, corruption)
Activation steps with responsible parties and required quorum
Copy integrity verification before use
Estimated execution time per scenario

XVI_3.titulo: Result of Last Redundancy Recovery Drill
XVI_3.descricao: Report of the last recovery exercise using the redundancy system, with scenario, participants, recovery time and result.
XVI_3.justificativa_legal: Clause XVII of IN 701 requires recovery procedures. The drill documents that these procedures work in practice, not just on paper.
conteudo_minimo: Date and scenario of the exercise
Participants and roles exercised
Recovery time measured vs. objective
Key integrity verified after recovery
Findings and improvements

XVI_4.descricao: Periodic report on the status of HSMs used: status of each unit, firmware version, last failover test and replacement timeline.
XVI_4.justificativa_legal: Physical redundancy depends on HSM status. A status report demonstrates that the redundancy infrastructure is operational and not merely configured.
conteudo_minimo: Number of HSMs per location and status
Firmware version and applied patches
Last failover test and result
Warranty period and replacement plan

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP uses Shamir Secret Sharing with shares distributed across 5 geographic locations in 3 different countries, with quarterly tested failover and key recovery time < 4h. No single location has sufficient keys to compromise the system.
indicadores: Shares distributed across minimum 5 geographic locations (3 countries)
Quarterly tested failover
Recovery time < 4h documented in tests
No single location with sufficient quorum
HSMs with updated firmware and within warranty

XVII.resumo: Documented recovery procedures for redundancy materials (clause XVI), specifically for BCB supervision in the event of intervention or liquidation.
XVII.gatilho: Mandatory together with clause XVI for VASPs with own custody.

XVII_1.descricao: Specific procedure for transferring key control to the BCB (or its designated entity) in the event of intervention, liquidation or regulatory request.
XVII_1.justificativa_legal: Clause XVII literally requires 'recovery procedures for materials subject to redundancy for BCB supervision purposes'. This is the only clause that explicitly names the BCB as recipient.
conteudo_minimo: Triggers: BCB intervention, liquidation, supervision request
Activation process with defined quorum
Steps for delivering redundancy material to BCB or its representative
Integrity verification before delivery
Delivery process record

XVII_2.descricao: Documentation demonstrating that the BCB delivery procedure was validated or communicated (where applicable), or that it is integrated into the wind-down plan.
XVII_2.justificativa_legal: Clause XVII explicitly targets BCB supervision. Evidence that the BCB has knowledge of the procedure (or that it is in the recovery plan) reinforces compliance.
conteudo_minimo: Reference to the supervision procedure in the wind-down plan
Or: confirmation of BCB validation
Location and accessibility of the redundancy material

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP has the BCB delivery procedure formally validated with specialised legal counsel, integrated into the wind-down plan, with an annual simulation of material delivery to an authorised third party. The BCB can access the material within 24h of a formal request.
indicadores: Procedure formally validated by legal counsel
Integrated into wind-down plan with annual simulation
BCB can access material within 24h
Material identified in a secure and accessible location

par1_I.tema: Clear and Accurate Information to Clients
par1_I.resumo: Obligation to provide clear and accurate information to the client about the institution, channels, contracted parties, insurance, rights, custody, asset and DLT, and staking operations.

par1_I_1.titulo: Membership Agreement / Terms of Use with Arts. 67 and 68 Information
par1_I_1.descricao: Current membership agreement or terms of use, containing all information required by arts. 67 and 68 of Res. 520 in clauses a–g of §1, I of IN 701.
par1_I_1.justificativa_legal: §1, I of IN 701 requires information about the institution, channels, contracted parties, insurance (art. 68), rights, custody, asset and DLT (art. 67). The contract/terms of use is the primary disclosure instrument.
conteudo_minimo: Full VASP identification (legal name, CNPJ, address, BCB authorisation)
Customer service channels and response SLA
Relevant contracted parties and responsibilities
Insurance information or declaration of absence (art. 68)
Client rights: withdrawals, portability, account closure
Characteristics of offered VA and inherent risks
Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) and its impacts
Current fees and charges (link to updated table)

par1_I_2.titulo: Current Insurance Policy or Formal Declaration of Absence
par1_I_2.descricao: Current insurance policy covering custody/operational risks (if existing), or formal VASP declaration that no insurance coverage exists and client VA are not covered by the FGC.
par1_I_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 68 of Res. 520 and clause (f) of §1, I of IN 701: the existence or absence of insurance must be disclosed. The policy (or formal declaration) is evidence that the client information is truthful.
conteudo_minimo: If insured: policy with covered risks, insured amount and validity
If uninsured: formal declaration with absence of FGC information
Information availability to the client (contract, portal)

par1_I_3.descricao: Current VASP fee and charge table, published in an easily accessible location (portal, app, terms of use), with effective date.
par1_I_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 67, IV of Res. 520: fee and charge disclosure. The published table allows the client to compare and make an informed decision — and demonstrates regulatory transparency.
conteudo_minimo: Transaction fees (buy, sell, swap)
Custody fees (if applicable)
Withdrawal and deposit fees (fiat and crypto)
Effective date and communication method for changes

par1_I_4.titulo: Staking-Specific Term with Full Risk Disclosure (art. 71)
par1_I_4.descricao: Specific staking term or dedicated section in the terms of use, covering all risks and conditions of art. 71.
par1_I_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 71 of Res. 520 and clause (h) of §1, I of IN 701 (par1_I_h): specific staking risk disclosure is an autonomous obligation. A separate term ensures clarity.
conteudo_minimo: Definition of the staking service offered
Lock-up period by protocol and early unstaking conditions
Slashing risk: definition, probability, consequences and VASP coverage
Liquidity risk during the lock-up period
Staking service fees (reward sharing, fixed fees)

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP makes all arts. 67 and 68 information available on a bilingual portal (PT and EN), even before login, with hyperlinks to the fee table, staking terms and insurance policy. Information is updated in real time and the client is notified by email and push when there is a material change.
indicadores: Information available before login (no registration required)
Automatic client notification on material changes
Real-time fee table updated via API
Staking term with full art. 71 disclosure approved by legal
Insurance policy or formal declaration updated annually

par1_II.tema: Financial Education and Best Practices Disclosure
par1_II.resumo: Provide clients with informational content on best practices and risks in VA market operations, in an accessible and updated format.

par1_II_1.descricao: URL and screenshot of the VASP's help centre or financial education page, with content on VA risks and security best practices.
par1_II_1.justificativa_legal: §1, II of IN 701: disclosure of informational content on best practices and risks. The existence of a dedicated channel (URL, screenshot) is the basic compliance evidence.
conteudo_minimo: VA risks: volatility, irreversibility, no FGC coverage
Security best practices: phishing, 2FA, strong passwords
Custody risks: responsibility for private keys
Complaint process and service channels
Update upon relevant emerging risks (e.g.: newly identified scam)

par1_II_2.titulo: VA Risk Information Material Available Before Login
par1_II_2.descricao: Evidence (screenshot with date) that the informational material on VA risks is accessible on the VASP's public page, before login.
par1_II_2.justificativa_legal: §1, II must be accessible to the client — even before becoming a client. Making it available before login maximises disclosure and demonstrates proactiveness.
conteudo_minimo: Public URL accessible without login
Minimum content on VA risks
Screenshot date and content's last update date

par1_II_3.descricao: Internal policy or procedure defining responsibility, minimum review frequency and update triggers for financial education content.
par1_II_3.justificativa_legal: Financial education content must be current and relevant. An update policy ensures the VASP keeps the content alive and not just a static outdated page.
conteudo_minimo: Financial education content owner
Minimum review frequency (at least annual)
Urgent update triggers (new scams, regulatory changes)
Approval process before publication

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP has a VA education academy with free courses, weekly articles on risks and best practices, and a proactive communication system (email/push) when new scams targeting the segment are detected. Content is produced by a dedicated editorial team and reviewed by compliance before publication.
indicadores: Content available on public page before login
Minimum monthly content update on risks
Proactive client communication about new scams/risks
Formal content owner with approval process
Content access metrics monitored

par1_III.resumo: Provide the client with an updated report of their VA position, with all fields required by art. 79 of Res. 520, in an accessible format and with full history.

par1_III_1.descricao: Template or anonymised sample of the position report issued to the client, demonstrating coverage of all art. 79 fields.
par1_III_1.justificativa_legal: §1, III of IN 701 and art. 79 of Res. 520: the position report is an explicit obligation. A sample proves the report exists, is available and contains the required fields.
conteudo_minimo: Client identification (anonymised in the sample)
VA held: type, quantity and reference value
Period movements (deposits, withdrawals, fees)
Report reference date and time
Availability: real-time portal and periodic delivery

par1_III_2.descricao: Screenshot (with date) of the client portal showing the statement/position report access menu, demonstrating ease of access.
par1_III_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 79 of Res. 520: the report must be available to the client. The portal screenshot proves the client has direct access without needing to request it.
conteudo_minimo: Portal screen with visible statement/position menu
Screenshot date
Demonstration that no request is needed — self-service access
