par1_III_3.descricao: Procedure defining how the client can contest a discrepancy in the position report and how the VASP investigates and resolves the issue.
par1_III_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 79, §2 of Res. 520: discrepancies between declared and actual position must have a treatment process. Without a procedure, the VASP cannot demonstrate it resolves errors in an organised manner.
conteudo_minimo: Client contestation channel
VASP response deadline (SLA)
Internal investigation process
Resolution and client communication

par1_III_4.titulo: Position Report Historical Retention Policy (5 years)
par1_III_4.descricao: Retention policy defining how and for how long position reports are stored, ensuring access by the client and BCB for 5 years.
par1_III_4.justificativa_legal: Art. 45, II and art. 79, §3 of Res. 520: position report history must be retained for 5 years. Without a retention policy, data may be lost and the VASP cannot meet BCB requests.
conteudo_minimo: Retention period: 5 years (art. 45, II)
Storage format and data integrity
Client access to history
BCB access to historical records

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP makes the position report available in real time via portal, API and scheduled email, with a complete 5-year history accessible in self-service. The client can export their full statement in CSV/PDF at any time. Discrepancies are resolved within 24h with proactive client communication.
indicadores: Real-time report via portal, API and email
5-year history accessible in self-service
CSV/PDF export available to client at any time
Discrepancy resolution SLA: 24h
Zero periods without report available to client

par1_I_h.resumo: Specific disclosure to the client of the conditions and risks of virtual asset staking operations, pursuant to art. 71 of Res. 520.
par1_I_h.gatilho: Triggered when the VASP offers a staking service to clients.

par1_I_h_1.titulo: Staking Term with Full art. 71 Disclosure
par1_I_h_1.descricao: Specific staking term or dedicated section in the terms of use, covering all risks and conditions of art. 71.
par1_I_h_1.justificativa_legal: Art. 71 of Res. 520 and clause (h) of §1, I of IN 701 (par1_I_h): specific staking risk disclosure is an autonomous obligation. A separate term ensures that disclosure is not lost in the general terms.
conteudo_minimo: Definition of the service and staking protocol(s) involved
Lock-up period by protocol and early unstaking conditions
Slashing risk: definition, probability, consequences and VASP coverage
Liquidity risk during the lock-up period
Staking service fees (reward sharing, fixed fees)
Reward treatment: crediting frequency, reinvestment
Protocol risk: fork, upgrade, discontinuation
Opt-out right and exit process

par1_I_h_2.descricao: Internal procedure defining how the VASP detects, responds and communicates a slashing event to the client in the validators operating for the staking service.
par1_I_h_2.justificativa_legal: Art. 71, III of Res. 520: the VASP must disclose the slashing risk. A management procedure proves the VASP is prepared for the event and the client will not be surprised without a treatment process.
conteudo_minimo: Validator monitoring system (slashing detection)
Slashing loss quantification process
VASP responsibility: absorption or pass-through to client
Client communication within X hours after the event
Compensation process (if the VASP assumes slashing risk)

par1_I_h_3.titulo: Staking Rewards History and Report by Protocol
par1_I_h_3.descricao: Periodic staking rewards report by protocol and per client, demonstrating consistency and traceability.
par1_I_h_3.justificativa_legal: Art. 79 of Res. 520 (position report) combined with the staking service: the client must have access to rewards history. A report demonstrates the process is transparent.
conteudo_minimo: Staking protocol and reference period
Amount of VA staked
Rewards generated and credited
VASP fee deducted
Relevant events in the period (slashing, fork)

resposta_otima.descricao: The ideal VASP has validators operated by a dedicated technical team with uptime > 99.9%, slashing insurance contracted to cover up to 100% of any event, 24x7 validator monitoring with real-time alerts, and a rewards report available to the client in the portal with daily granularity.
indicadores: Own validators with > 99.9% uptime
Slashing insurance covering 100% of the event
24x7 monitoring with real-time slashing alert
Daily granularity rewards report in the portal
Client communication within 1h of any slashing event
